summary:
- **Core purpose/topic:** Audit and transmission of the **Annual Abstract Account** and related financial statements for the **Straits Settlements**, with the **Director of Colonial Audit** reporting on accuracy, classification issues, and compliance with Colonial Regulations.  
- **Geography & period:** **Straits Settlements (incl. Singapore, Penang/Province Wellesley, Malacca; also references to Labuan and Christmas Island)**; accounts for **years ending 31 Dec 1939 and 31 Dec 1940**, with audit/covering correspondence dated **1941–1942**.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Director of Colonial Audit (A. J. Harding)**; **Accountant-General W. C. Hodges**; **Auditor, S.S. & F.M.S.**; **Colonial Office (Downing Street)**; **Governor Sir Shenton Thomas**; despatch signed **Moyne**; internal notes mention **W. Morrison**, **D.C.A.**, and **Jo Stratton**.  
- **Major audit observations (1940):** Misclassification errors noted too late to correct (two **$10** items charged to **Revenue Head 2 “Licences”** instead of **Expenditure Head 33 “Medical, Health Branch”**); **Deposits liability $13,361,761.85** included **$1,104,575.99** for the **Aliens Immigration Fund** not yet reconciled at time of report; **boards of survey** not held in 1940 for stores of **Drainage & Irrigation Department** and **Penang & Province Wellesley Volunteer Force**.  
- **Public debt/war finance omissions (1940):** Statement of Public Debt (per **Colonial Regulation 354(v)**) lacked details of a **1940 loan under Ordinance No. 32 of 1940** and **War Savings Certificates under Ordinance No. 33 of 1940**; loan realised **$25,000,000** for **His Majesty’s Government war purposes**; War Savings Certificates issued to **31 Dec 1940: $2,260,095**, with **$1,090,155** handed to the **Imperial Government** as a war gift.  
- **Supply Ordinance / approvals (1940):** The audit report (dated **20 Jan 1942**) stated the **Final Supply Ordinance for 1940** was not yet enacted, but later internal note cites the **Gazette of 5 Dec 1941** that it received the **Governor’s assent on 27 Nov 1941**; uncertainty/absence noted about **Message No. 26 (1940)** and approval for additional expenditure.  
- **Notable financial totals (1940):** Total receipts (excluding opening balance) **$199,080,678.72**; total payments **$199,454,491.78**; **balance 31 Dec 1940: $6,339,390.16**; total revenue **$49,172,604.14** vs estimated **$38,203,678.00**; total expenditure **$59,371,338.35** vs estimated **$45,291,271.00** (large overrun shown under “Miscellaneous Services”).
